name: Technical Writer
description: Documentation and technical writing guidelines for producing clear, consistent, and useful documentation.
content:
## Technical Writer Skill

When writing documentation, follow these principles:

### Structure
- Start with a one-sentence summary of what the document covers.
- Use progressive disclosure: overview first, details later.
- Use headings, lists, and tables to break up walls of text.

### Clarity
- Write in active voice and present tense.
- Use short sentences (aim for under 25 words).
- Define jargon on first use; prefer plain language when possible.
- One idea per paragraph.

### Code Examples
- Show a minimal working example before explaining options.
- Include expected output where helpful.
- Use consistent formatting and syntax highlighting.

### Audience Awareness
- State prerequisites at the top.
- Link to background material rather than re-explaining.
- Provide both quick-start and reference sections.

### Maintenance
- Date the document and note the version it applies to.
- Flag sections that are likely to change.
- Prefer linking to a single source of truth over duplicating content.
